s: What's the most embarrassing thing you've done at a party? Take a sip if you'd rather not say.
z: What's the most embarrassing thing you've done at a party? Do 10 jumping jacks if you'd rather not say.
f: Do 10 jumping jacks instead.

s: What's a lie you've told this week? Sip your drink if you won't come clean.
z: What's a lie you've told this week? Touch your nose if you won't come clean.
f: Touch your nose instead.

s: What's the most childish thing you still secretly enjoy?
Name something you've done that you'd never post on social media. Sip if you skip.
z: Name something you've done that you'd never post on social media. Say 'no comment' dramatically if you skip.
f: Say 'no comment' dramatically instead.

s: What's the weirdest food combination you actually enjoy?
Have you ever pretended to like a gift you hated? Describe it, or take a drink.
z: Have you ever pretended to like a gift you hated? Describe it, or do your best fake-happy face.
f: Do your best fake-happy face instead.

s: What's the most awkward date you've been on? Spill or sip.
z: What's the most awkward date you've been on? Spill or you must compliment the person to your left.
f: Compliment the person to your left instead.

s: Have you ever walked into the wrong bathroom and just stayed?
What's something embarrassing you've Googled? Sip if you won't say.
z: What's something embarrassing you've Googled? Do 5 squats if you won't say.
f: Do 5 squats instead.

s: What's a weird habit you have when no one's watching?
What's the most cringe text you've ever sent? Read it aloud or take a sip.
z: What's the most cringe text you've ever sent? Read it aloud or sing a line from your most-played song.
f: Sing a line from your most-played song instead.

s: What's the worst excuse you've used to cancel plans?
What's the most overrated thing everyone seems to love?
Have you ever lied about reading a book or watching a show everyone talks about? Sip if yes.
z: Have you ever lied about reading a book or watching a show everyone talks about? Do a little bow if yes.
f: Do a little bow instead.

s: What's a skill you always claim you have but secretly don't?
What's the most embarrassing thing you've done because you were nervous?
Have you ever been caught talking to yourself? What were you saying? Sip if you won't share.
z: Have you ever been caught talking to yourself? What were you saying? Explain what you were doing instead if you won't share.
f: Explain what you were doing instead.

s: What's the pettiest thing you've ever done to get back at someone?
What's something you believe that most people in this room would disagree with?
What's your most embarrassing autocorrect story? Tell it or take a sip.
z: What's your most embarrassing autocorrect story? Tell it or do a robot impression.
f: Do a robot impression instead.

s: What's the most embarrassing thing a family member has done in public?
Have you ever pretended to be busy just to avoid someone you know? Sip if yes.
z: Have you ever pretended to be busy just to avoid someone you know? Say 'I plead the fifth' if yes.
f: Say 'I plead the fifth' instead.

s: What's the last white lie you told?
What's something you've bought that you'd be embarrassed to admit was a waste of money?
What's your go-to excuse for being late?
Name a show or movie you secretly love but never admit to watching. Sip if you won't confess.
z: Name a show or movie you secretly love but never admit to watching. Describe it without naming it if you won't confess.
f: Describe it without naming it instead.

s: Have you ever fallen asleep somewhere embarrassing? Where?
What's your most irrational fear?
What's the worst fashion choice you ever made?
What's something you've done that you thought no one saw — but someone probably did?
Do your best impression of the person to your left. They rate it out of 10.
Let someone in the group text one message from your phone. They choose the recipient.
Speak in an accent for the next 3 cards.
Show the most recent photo in your camera roll to everyone.
Call or text someone in your contacts and wish them a happy birthday — right now.
Do your best celebrity impression. Group has 10 seconds to guess who it is.
Switch seats with the person who voted for you last round. If no vote round happened, swap with someone wearing the most colour.
Describe your day so far, but you can only use emojis and sound effects.
Let the group give you a new name for the rest of the game.
Do 10 jumping jacks while singing a song the group picks.
Hold a plank for 20 seconds.
Tell a joke. If no one laughs, take a drink.
z: Tell a joke. If no one laughs, do a dramatic bow.
f: Do a dramatic bow instead if no one laughs.

s: Let the person to your right choose your phone wallpaper for the next 5 minutes.
Post a selfie with a caption the group decides. Delete it after 30 minutes.
Narrate everything you do for the next 2 cards in third person.
Do your best catwalk down the room and back.
Let the group scroll through your Spotify (or music app) for 10 seconds.
Talk without using the letter 'S' for the next 2 minutes.
Everyone gets to ask you one yes/no question. You must answer every one honestly.
Do your best slow-motion action of something dramatic. Group rates it.
Try to lick your elbow. If you succeed, everyone else takes a drink.
z: Try to lick your elbow. If you succeed, everyone else does 5 jumping jacks.
f: Everyone else does 5 jumping jacks instead.

s: Give a 30-second motivational speech about something completely trivial.
Show your most-used emoji keyboard to the group. No deleting anything first.
Talk like a sports commentator describing whatever the person to your right is doing right now.
Freestyle rap about someone in the room for 15 seconds.
Do your best robot impression for 30 seconds.
Let the person to your left draw a portrait of you — you cannot look until they're done.
Speak only in questions for the next 2 minutes.
Do your best impression of a TV show character without naming the show.
Stand up and do a 20-second victory dance like you just won something huge.
Who in the room is most likely to accidentally end up on the news?
Who in the room is most likely to still be awake at 4am?
Who in the room takes the longest to get ready? The winner takes a drink.
z: Who in the room takes the longest to get ready? The winner does 3 stretches.
f: Winner does 3 stretches instead.

s: Who in the room is most likely to text their ex after tonight?
Who in the room would survive longest in a zombie apocalypse?
Who in the room is most likely to accidentally reply-all to an email? Person with most votes takes a drink.
z: Who in the room is most likely to accidentally reply-all to an email? Person with most votes tells us their most embarrassing email story.
f: Winner tells their most embarrassing email story instead.

s: Who in the room would be the best villain in a movie?
Who in the room is most likely to order the wrong thing at a restaurant?
Who in the room gives the worst gift? Person with most votes takes a drink.
z: Who in the room gives the worst gift? Person with most votes must defend their gifting skills.
f: Person with most votes defends their gifting skills instead.

s: Who in the room would be the worst roommate?
Who in the room is most likely to get a tattoo they regret?
Who in the room would be most likely to ghost someone?
Who in the room is most likely to go viral for the wrong reason?
Who in the room would be most likely to start a cult — accidentally? The person with most votes takes a drink.
z: Who in the room would most likely start a cult accidentally? They must deliver a 10-second motivational speech.
f: Winner delivers a 10-second motivational speech instead.

s: Who in the room is most likely to accidentally sit in the wrong meeting?
Who in the room has the best poker face?
Who in the room is most likely to over-pack for a one-night trip?
Who in the room would make the best reality TV star?
Who in the room is most likely to be late to their own party? Person with most votes takes a drink.
z: Who in the room is most likely to be late to their own party? They apologise dramatically.
f: Person with most votes apologises dramatically instead.

s: Who in the room would be the most dramatic in a hospital waiting room?
Who in the room is most likely to start a business that somehow works out?
Who in the room is most likely to lie about their age?
Who in the room would survive a week in the wilderness? Person with fewest votes takes a drink.
z: Who in the room would survive a week in the wilderness? The least outdoorsy person must demonstrate their worst camping skill.
f: Least outdoorsy person demonstrates their worst camping skill instead.

s: Who in the room talks the most on the phone?
Who in the room is most likely to accidentally send a complaint email to the person they're complaining about?
Until the next rule card: everyone must refer to themselves in the third person.
Until the next rule card: no one is allowed to say 'I'. Anyone who does takes a drink.
z: Until the next rule card: no one is allowed to say 'I'. Anyone who does must clap once.
f: Anyone who breaks it claps once instead.

s: Until the next rule card: everyone must end every sentence with 'allegedly'.
Until the next rule card: no one can point using their finger — use an elbow only. Anyone who forgets takes a drink.
z: Anyone who points with their finger must do a little spin instead.
f: Anyone who breaks it does a little spin instead.

s: Until the next rule card: no swearing allowed. Anyone who slips takes a drink.
z: Until the next rule card: no swearing allowed. Anyone who slips must say the word again in a funny voice.
f: Anyone who slips says it again in a funny voice instead.

s: Until the next rule card: you must say 'with great power comes great responsibility' before every answer.
Until the next rule card: everyone must whisper. Anyone who speaks at normal volume takes a drink.
z: Until the next rule card: everyone must whisper. Anyone who forgets must cover their mouth for 30 seconds.
f: Anyone who speaks at normal volume covers their mouth for 30 seconds instead.

s: Until the next rule card: everyone must give a thumbs up before speaking.
Until the next rule card: you must do jazz hands whenever you make a point.
Until the next rule card: no one is allowed to use the word 'no'. Penalty: drink.
z: Until the next rule card: no one can say 'no'. Penalty: wave your hands dramatically.
f: Anyone who says 'no' waves dramatically instead.

s: Until the next rule card: the person to your left is your lawyer. You must consult them before answering any question.
Until the next rule card: speak only in questions. Any statement = drink.
z: Until the next rule card: speak only in questions. Any statement = stand up and sit back down.
f: Any statement made, person stands and sits instead.

s: Until the next rule card: the last person to take a drink must compliment the next person who speaks.
Until the next rule card: everyone must do a little bow before speaking.
Until the next rule card: no names allowed. Refer to people by a nickname the group just made up.
Until the next rule card: anyone who laughs must take a drink.
z: Until the next rule card: anyone who laughs must do a salute.
f: Anyone who laughs does a salute instead.

s: Until the next rule card: all questions must be answered with a question.
Until the next rule card: no one may cross their arms or legs. Penalty: drink.
z: Until the next rule card: no one may cross their arms or legs. Penalty: stand for 30 seconds.
f: Anyone caught crossing arms/legs stands for 30 seconds instead.

s: Until the next rule card: everyone must start every sentence with 'Interestingly enough...'
Until the next rule card: the highest-energy person becomes the game host and introduces every card.
Until the next rule card: whenever someone says a number, everyone must snap.
Until the next rule card: anyone who checks their phone takes a drink.
z: Until the next rule card: anyone who checks their phone must tell everyone what they were looking for.
f: Anyone who checks their phone tells everyone what they were looking for instead.

s: Until the next rule card: all eye contact must be maintained for at least 3 seconds before speaking.
Until the next rule card: everything must be said as if you're revealing shocking news.
Until the next rule card: no first names. Everyone picks a title: 'The Honourable,' 'Captain,' etc.
Everyone who has ever lied to get out of going to work or school — take a sip.
z: Everyone who has ever lied to get out of going to work or school — stand up.
f: Stand up instead.

s: Everyone who has sent a 'we need to talk' message just to send something funny — take a sip.
z: Everyone who has done this — do a little shimmy.
f: Do a little shimmy instead.

s: Everyone who has walked into a room and completely forgotten why — sip.
z: Everyone who has done this — raise your hand and say 'same'.
f: Raise your hand and say 'same' instead.

s: Everyone who has pretended not to see someone in public — sip.
z: Everyone who has done this — mime a phone call.
f: Mime a phone call instead.

s: Everyone who still watches a show that was cancelled years ago — sip.
z: Everyone who does this — name the show right now.
f: Name the show right now instead.

s: Everyone who has googled symptoms and convinced themselves they had something serious — sip.
z: Everyone who has done this — say the disease you convinced yourself you had.
f: Say the disease you convinced yourself you had instead.

s: Everyone who has laughed so hard they snorted — sip.
z: Everyone who has done this — recreate the snort right now.
f: Recreate the snort right now instead.

s: Everyone who has accidentally liked something very old on someone's social media — sip.
z: Everyone who has done this — own it and explain what you were looking for.
f: Own it and explain what you were doing instead.

s: Everyone who has cried at a commercial — sip.
z: Everyone who has cried at a commercial — name the commercial (bonus: re-enact the ending).
f: Name the commercial instead.

s: Everyone who has ever eaten food that fell on the floor (3-second rule) — sip.
z: Everyone who has done this — hold up their hand proudly.
f: Hold up your hand proudly instead.

s: Everyone who has binge-watched an entire season in one day — sip.
z: Everyone who has done this — name what you watched.
f: Name what you watched instead.

s: Everyone who has a food guilty pleasure that would embarrass them — sip and confess.
z: Everyone who has this — confess it anyway.
f: Confess it anyway, no drink required.

s: Everyone who still doesn't know all the words to their favourite song — sip.
z: Everyone who doesn't — try singing a verse now and show how badly they do it.
f: Attempt a verse and show how badly you do it instead.

s: Everyone who has stayed in a conversation they desperately wanted to leave — sip.
z: Everyone who has done this — give a little trapped-smile demonstration.
f: Give a little trapped-smile demonstration instead.

s: Everyone who has ever replied 'I'm almost there' when they hadn't left yet — sip.
z: Everyone who has done this — raise your hand and say 'guilty'.
f: Raise your hand and say 'guilty' instead.

s: Everyone who has a secret celebrity crush you've never told anyone — sip, then say a clue.
z: Everyone who has one — give a clue without saying the name.
f: Give a clue without a drink instead.

s: Everyone who has tried to cut their own hair and regretted it — sip.
z: Everyone who has done this — describe the aftermath.
f: Describe the aftermath instead.

s: Everyone who has fallen asleep during a movie at the cinema — sip.
z: Everyone who has done this — name the movie.
f: Name the movie instead.

s: Everyone who still sometimes sleeps with a light on — sip.
z: Everyone who does this — give a thumbs up.
f: Give a thumbs up instead.

s: Everyone who has talked to an animal as if it could understand them — sip.
z: Everyone who has done this — demonstrate what they said.
f: Demonstrate what you said instead.

s: {P} — pick someone in the room to reenact your best drunk story. They must perform it.
{P} — describe {P2} using only food. {P2} rates the accuracy.
{P} — do your best impression of {P2}. Group votes if it's good. If fewer than half agree, {P} takes a drink.
z: If fewer than half agree, {P} must do it again but funnier.
f: If fewer than half agree, do it again but funnier instead.

s: {P1} and {P2} — you have 30 seconds to come up with a handshake. Perform it for the group.
{P} — give {P2} a compliment so over-the-top the group groans. If they don't groan, {P} takes a drink.
z: If they don't groan, {P} must try again even more dramatically.
f: Try again even more dramatically instead.

s: {P} — describe your ideal Sunday without using the words 'sleep', 'Netflix', or 'food'.
{P} — stare into {P2}'s eyes for 30 seconds without laughing. If either person laughs, they take a drink.
z: If either person laughs, they must say 'I'm so weak' to the group.
f: If either person laughs, say 'I'm so weak' to the group instead.

s: {P} — you have 1 minute to teach the group something you actually know how to do.
{P} — pick the person you think has the messiest room right now. They take a drink.
z: The person {P} picks must defend the state of their room.
f: The person picked must defend the state of their room instead.

s: {P} — swap shoes with {P2} for the next 3 cards.
{P} — tell {P2} the most genuine compliment you can. Group votes if it was heartfelt. If it wasn't, {P} takes a drink.
z: If the group doesn't buy it, {P} tries again sincerely.
f: If the group doesn't buy it, try again sincerely instead.

s: {P} — pick the person in the room you'd most want on your team in a trivia game. That person takes a drink as a winner's toast.
z: The chosen person gets to ask any yes/no question of their choice instead.
f: The chosen person gets to ask a yes/no question instead.

s: {P1} and {P2} — debate which is better: dogs or cats. 60 seconds, winner decided by the group.
{P} — pick one person in the room to give a totally made-up award. Announce it like an Oscar.
{P} — tell the group a true story about {P2} that {P2} wouldn't have chosen to share tonight.
{P} — guess what {P2} spent their last big purchase on. If wrong, {P} takes a drink.
z: If wrong, {P} must do 5 squats.
f: If wrong, do 5 squats instead.

s: {P1} — pose for a photo with {P2} as if you're in a terrible stock photo. Group judges.
{P} — pick the person you'd trust most in a hostage negotiation. That person must demonstrate their negotiating skills right now.
{P} — tell the room one fact about {P2} that most people don't know. {P2} confirms or denies.
{P} — impersonate how {P2} acts when they're trying to be impressive. {P2} rates the accuracy from 1-10.
